narrative:
Lionnel Mpasi Nzau is the Democratic Republic of the Congo's goalkeeper, boasting an overall rating of 7.12/10. This places him in the top 10% of goalkeepers at this tournament. His rating reflects both his club form and World Cup contributions.

Mpasi Nzau has played 360 minutes at this World Cup, demonstrating his importance to the team. He averages 3.5 saves per 90 minutes, highlighting his shot-stopping ability. Additionally, he records 1.5 clearances per 90 minutes, indicating his involvement beyond pure shot-stopping.

As a key figure, Mpasi Nzau anchors his national team's defense from the goalkeeping position. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his consistent saving ability, which accounts for much of his tournament impact. His high overall rating, largely driven by 88% club form, suggests a reliable presence to watch.
